title: Disposable multi-instance demo stacks
acceptance: M3-D5: one demo stack (postgres+redis) isolated alongside the dev anthropos stack — up/status/down, dev untouched. Verified live on a 16 GB box.

decisions.M3-D3: clone at latest release tag, not main
decisions.M3-D4: tooling home = own gitignored anthropos-demo/demo-stacks repo; skills+guide in rosetta
decisions.M3-D5: acceptance adjusted to 1 stack alongside dev (16 GB host can't fit 2 full stacks)

injection_status: S3 is WIRING SCAFFOLD only — NOT verified on live services. Proven: the publishable-key mint (format-identical to clerkenstein's gated impl). Unverified: clerk-backend/authn/clerk-webhook recipes (artifacts emitted, not run). Unbuilt: the patched-colony module the authn go.mod-replace needs (clerkenstein ships the authn twin package, not a colony module).
resource_gated: the `app` (Clerk consumer) needs the full graphql profile (~10-12 GB; hard depends_on skiller/skillpath/...) — doesn't fit Docker's ~8 GB VM beside the dev stack. So injection can't be verified here.
carried_forward: M3-CF1 — assemble the patched-colony module + verify all 4 injection recipes end-to-end (demo app accepts a Clerkenstein token + browser login) on the full stack / a bigger Docker VM. Real integration work, not just a hardware wait.
dev_stack_safety: every op -p demo-N scoped; down hard-refuses the dev project; verified the dev stack (12 containers) stayed intact across demo-1's lifecycle AND the failed app-bring-up attempt
